rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RABENER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Harley-Davidson, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Rabener was exposed to original equipment brakes attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Johnson precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Mr. Hejlik¿s deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether the original equipment brakes attributable to defendant that Mr. Rabener was exposed to contained asbestos. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Rabener¿s exposure to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Rabener¿s asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Rabener and Mr. Hejlik are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WILSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 8, DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Cohen¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication failed to state what purported undisputed material facts applied to any of the issues sought to be summarily adjudicated. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. DEFENDANT ATLAS HEATING & VENTILATING COMPANY, LTD. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the fireproofing disturbed by defendant in his presence contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
